title: NOC 14402 – Production Logistics Workers | GTR Immigration
description: Production Logistics Workers play a vital role in keeping manufacturing, construction, and publishing operations running smoothly by coordinating materials, tracking production flow, and managing supply schedules across industrial environments in Canada. Classified under NOC 14402, this occupation has seen strong post-pandemic hiring growth and is expected to remain in steady demand—creating practical immigration pathways through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.teer: Expansion of domestic manufacturingGrowth in infrastructure projectsEntry-level TEER occupation with technical career pathwaysEmployer-supported recruitmentSupply-chain optimization initiatives
classification.major_group: 144 – Supply chain logistics, tracking, and scheduling coordination occupations
classification.minor_group: 1440 – Supply chain logistics, tracking, and scheduling coordination occupations

duties: Production Logistics Workers may perform some or all of the following:
Move and track goods between departments and production units
Perform quality-control checks on packed items
Prepare reports on workflow progress and production issues
Monitor inventory levels and order schedules
Maintain vendor contact to ensure timely delivery of supplies
Estimate material needs for production projects
employment_requirements: Familiarity with inventory systems and logistics software
Experience in manufacturing or warehouse environments (an asset)
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create approximately 10,300 new positions for Production Logistics Workers, while about 7,400 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and targeted immigration planning especially valuable for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Production Logistics Workers are in demand across manufacturing, construction, and publishing sectors nationwide. Hiring has expanded rapidly since the pandemic and is expected to continue over the coming years.
Manufacturing and assembly plantsConstruction companiesPrinting and publishing housesIndustrial warehousesLarge-scale production facilities
Annual salaries typically range between $36,600 and $79,000 CAD, depending on province, experience, and specialization.
